problem: The hlx CLI has zero support for ticket relationships (afterTicketId, referencedTicketIds, implementFromTicketId) despite the server API and web UI fully supporting them. Users cannot create tickets with dependency chains, cross-references, or research-to-implementation links from the CLI, nor can they see relationship data when viewing tickets.

files.why: Primary change target. Builds POST body for ticket creation (line 89-93). Currently sends only title, description, repositoryIds, mode. Must add --after, --reference, --implement-from flag parsing, resolveTicket() calls, and include resolved IDs in body. Also needs error handling wrap around hxFetch call.

Display target. TicketDetail type (lines 5-23) omits all relationship fields. printTicketDetail() (line 47-87) must add 'Depends on' / 'Implements' / 'References' display sections after approval status and before Repositories.
Display target. TicketItem type (lines 5-13) omits relationship fields. Output line (line 107) must append [after RSH-XXX] indicator when afterTicket is present.
Help/usage text. ticketsUsage() (line 15-33) and case 'create' help (line 71-77) must include new --after, --reference, --implement-from flags in usage strings.
Exported documentation. 'hlx tickets create' flags table (lines 99-107) must add three new flag rows. Worked Examples section needs relationship examples.
Agent skill documentation. Ticket Management workflow section (lines 74-93) needs relationship command examples.
Command reference. 'hlx tickets create' in Action Commands (line 56) must include new relationship flags.

Existing utility to reuse. resolveTicket() (lines 86-167) handles ID/shortId/numeric resolution with active+archived fallback. Will be imported into create.ts for reference resolution. No changes needed to this file.
Existing utility to reuse. getFlag() (lines 5-9) for optional flag parsing. Already imported in create.ts. No changes needed to this file.

Existing HTTP client. hxFetch() throws Error with message containing HTTP status and JSON body on failure (line 34: 'HTTP {status} {statusText} - {body}'). Error handling in create.ts must extract the JSON error field from this message format.

Existing test file for resolveTicket(). Contains 15 test cases covering ID/shortId/numeric resolution, ambiguity, archived fallback, and error propagation. No changes needed but confirms resolveTicket() is well-tested.
Build/quality gates. Scripts: build='tsc', typecheck='tsc --noEmit', test='tsc && node --test dist/**/*.test.js'. Only devDeps are @types/node and typescript. No ORM, no migrations.
TypeScript config. target=ES2022, module=Node16, strict=true, declaration=true. All source in src/, output in dist/.

facts.claim: helix-cli has zero references to afterTicketId, referencedTicketIds, or implementFromTicketId in source code
facts.evidence: Grep across helix-cli codebase returned zero source file matches. Only match is in .helix/tickets/*/ticket.md (the research report itself).

facts.claim: Server API Zod schema already accepts afterTicketId, implementFromTicketId, and referencedTicketIds fields
facts.evidence: helix-global-server/src/controllers/ticket-controller.ts lines 29-40: createTicketSchemaGeneral includes afterTicketId (optional string), implementFromTicketId (optional string), referencedTicketIds (optional array, max 5).

facts.claim: create.ts POST body currently sends only title, description, repositoryIds, and optional mode
facts.evidence: helix-cli/src/tickets/create.ts lines 89-93: body: { title, description, repositoryIds, ...(mode && { mode }) }

facts.claim: TicketDetail type in get.ts omits all relationship fields
facts.evidence: helix-cli/src/tickets/get.ts lines 5-23: type includes id, shortId, title, description, status, branchName, reporter, repositories, runs, mergeQueueStatus, approvalStatus, isArchived. No afterTicket*, implementFromTicket*, or referencedTicket* fields.

facts.claim: TicketItem type in list.ts omits all relationship fields
facts.evidence: helix-cli/src/tickets/list.ts lines 5-13: type includes id, shortId, title, status, updatedAt, approvalStatus, reporter. No relationship fields.

facts.claim: resolveTicket() utility exists and handles 3 reference formats with active+archived fallback
facts.evidence: helix-cli/src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts lines 86-167: handles internal ID, short ID (case-insensitive), numeric ticket number. Fetches active first, falls back to archived. Detects cross-set ambiguity.

facts.claim: getFlag() utility exists for optional flag parsing
facts.evidence: helix-cli/src/lib/flags.ts lines 5-9: returns string | undefined based on args array indexOf.

facts.claim: hxFetch error format includes HTTP status and response body text
facts.evidence: helix-cli/src/lib/http.ts lines 28-35: buildErrorMessage returns 'HTTP {status} {statusText} - {body text (truncated to 500 chars)}'.

facts.claim: Existing resolve-then-use pattern in create.ts for --repos can be followed for new flags
facts.evidence: helix-cli/src/tickets/create.ts lines 60-76: reposRaw.split(',').map(s => s.trim()) pattern, try/catch with resolveAllRepos(), console.error + process.exit(1) on failure.

facts.claim: No ORM, no migrations, no database in helix-cli
facts.evidence: package.json devDependencies: only @types/node and typescript. No prisma, drizzle, typeorm, sequelize, or knex.

facts.claim: Only helix-cli requires code changes; server and client are context-only
facts.evidence: Research report Section 4 states 'Only helix-cli requires code changes.' Server API already supports all relationship fields. Web UI already supports all three relationship mechanisms.

facts.claim: Quality gates are: tsc (build), tsc --noEmit (typecheck), tsc && node --test dist/**/*.test.js (test)
facts.evidence: package.json scripts: build='tsc', typecheck='tsc --noEmit', test='tsc && node --test dist/**/*.test.js'

facts.claim: Three test files exist in the codebase
facts.evidence: Glob for **/*.test.ts found: src/lib/flags.test.ts, src/lib/resolve-ticket.test.ts, src/skill/skill.test.ts

unknowns.question: Does the hxFetch error thrown for 400-status POST responses actually include the JSON body inline in the error message?
unknowns.context: buildErrorMessage (http.ts line 28-35) reads response.text() and appends it. The research report assumes the error message format is 'HTTP 400 Bad Request - {"error":"..."}'. This should be verified at runtime but is plausible from static inspection of buildErrorMessage which appends the response body text.

unknowns.question: Are there any additional fields the server list endpoint returns for relationship data beyond what the research report documents?
unknowns.context: Research report cites helix-global-server/src/services/ticket-service.ts lines 1674-1709 for list response shape. Line numbers may have drifted since research was conducted. The Zod schema in ticket-controller.ts was verified, but the response mapping was not re-read at those exact lines.

unknowns.question: Should tests be added for the new create.ts flag parsing and display changes?
unknowns.context: The research report does not mention adding tests. Existing test files cover flags.ts and resolve-ticket.ts utilities but not the command functions themselves (create.ts, get.ts, list.ts). The test infrastructure uses node:test with mock support.

serverApiContext.note: Server API contract documented in research report Section 2 was verified against helix-global-server/src/controllers/ticket-controller.ts lines 29-40 (Zod schema). No server changes needed.
serverApiContext.createEndpoint: POST /api/tickets - accepts afterTicketId, implementFromTicketId, referencedTicketIds (all optional)
serverApiContext.detailEndpoint: GET /api/tickets/:id - returns nested afterTicket, implementFromTicket, referencedTickets objects
serverApiContext.listEndpoint: GET /api/tickets - returns afterTicketId, afterTicket (nested), implementFromTicketId, referencedTicketIds per item
